data.description:
# Feature-branch accumulation: multi-story features assemble off-main under per-story LLM+CI gates and land via a reviewed merge change

## Context
Validation (session log apt-otter-urn, 2026-07-01) proved the current chain/topic flow cannot satisfy the five core requirements for multi-agent feature work: (R1) never review a huge diff in one review; (R2) accumulate a whole feature off-main and land it at once; (R3) every change reaching main passed LLM review; (R4) no invalidation fragility from content comparison or merge/rebase instability; (R5) the pattern must be validated by popular, actively-maintained OSS projects. Chains fail R4 (any rebase wipes votes under copyCondition=NO_CODE_CHANGE; the alternative TRIVIAL_REBASE carry IS content comparison). The adopted pattern is the OpenDev-documented **server-side feature branch + reviewed merge change**: stories are small changes reviewed INTO refs/heads/feature/<name> as they pass (attestations are consumed immediately into branch history — no vote ever needs to survive a rebase; branch history is the durable accumulator), then one --no-ff merge commit of the branch into main is pushed to refs/for/main as its own reviewed change, gated identically, and submitted atomically. Feature branches live only in Gerrit (replication pushes main+tags), so nothing reaches GitHub until the merge change lands.

**ASSUMES epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) is completed and merged**: the `Verified` label exists, cast by GitHub Actions via gerrit-to-platform hooks (patchset-created / comment-added `recheck`), and the two-vote submit requirement `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND label:Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved` is active.

### Decision log (2026-07-01 validation + web research; citations in apt-otter-urn)
- **OSS validation (R5):** OpenDev Infra Manual documents this flow verbatim (server-side branch, pushMerge ACL, `git merge --no-ff` pushed to refs/for/master); live use: openstack/swift feature/mpu + feature/threaded through 2026 with story-sized reviewed changes + reviewed catch-up merges; Qt runs reviewed merge changes live (qt-creator, June 2026). Caveat adopted as policy: keep feature branches SHORT-LIVED (OpenDev: "specifically not for sustained long term development"; Qt retreated from routine long-lived-branch merges in 2020).
- **Merge-change review base = Gerrit auto-merge** (conflict resolutions + manual edits only — usually near-empty), NOT the whole feature diff (R1). REST: `files/` with no `parent` param and per-file `diff` default to the auto-merge base for merge commits; `mergelist` lists integrated commits. **TRAP:** bare `GET .../revisions/{rev}/patch` on a merge commit returns HTTP 409 ("Revision has more than 1 parent", GetPatch.java) — today our voter would fail-closed -1 on any merge change (safe, but blocks the pattern); `patch?parent=1` silently yields the ENTIRE feature diff (violates R1). The bot needs a distinct merge-change path.
- **UI default diff base for merge commits is FIRST_PARENT** (whole feature) — fine for humans; never design assuming reviewers land on the conflicts-only view.
- **LLM-Review copyCondition gains `changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`** — Gerrit-native, purpose-built ("useful if you don't want to trigger CI or human verification again if your target branch moved forward", config-labels.html). A re-merge because main advanced keeps the +1; a changed feature tip is a REWORK and correctly re-reviews. Not a content-comparison heuristic (R4).
- **Verified deliberately does NOT carry on re-merge** (stays NO_CODE_CHANGE only): CI re-runs on the new combined tree via patchset-created. This is the semantic-conflict mitigation — a textually-clean merge shows an EMPTY auto-merge diff even when semantically broken (Chromium's standing objection to merges), so the LLM cannot catch it; CI must. Deliberate label divergence mirroring roles: LLM reviews content (unchanged on a clean re-merge), CI validates the tree (changed).
- **Submit type pinned explicitly**: `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` (currently unset/inherited — an unpinned gap flagged in validation; FF-only or cherry-pick/rebase-always on main would defeat the atomic-merge design).
- `receive.rejectImplicitMerges` is unaffected — it explicitly exempts real merge commits (config-project-config.html).

## Locked design
- **Branch namespace** `refs/heads/feature/<name>`, created/deleted server-side (UI/REST); `Create Reference` + `Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` granted to a narrow group; `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` AND `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*` (catch-up merges are reviewed too). Branch deletion after merge-back is a scripted/manual step (Gerrit has no auto-cleanup).
- **Story loop:** small changes target `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/<name>`; the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic and the submit requirements have no branch clause, so BOTH votes gate every feature-branch change (R3); submit into the branch as each passes. Parallel agents in separate worktrees both target the branch; MERGE_IF_NECESSARY merges siblings without rebasing anyone (R4).
- **Catch-up merges** main→feature as reviewed merge changes on the branch (OpenDev recipe), on a documented cadence; branch-lifetime cap.
- **Merge-back:** `--no-ff` merge commit (Change-Id stamped via `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend`), pushed to refs/for/main under pushMerge; bot reviews vs auto-merge + mergelist; CI builds/tests the merge tree; both labels gate; submit lands the feature atomically; replication advances GitHub main; branch deleted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **S1 — Gate + ACL config** (`infra/gerrit/project.config`, `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`, declarative): LLM-Review `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`; Verified copyCondition UNCHANGED (re-run posture) with the rationale recorded; `[submit]` block pinning `action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true`; ACLs: `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*`, `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` + `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*`; ADR amending ADR-0013 (+ 1fa8's Verified ADR) recording the merge-change gate + label-divergence rationale; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` gate/ACL sections updated. Verified live on the running Gerrit.
- [ ] **S2 — Review-bot merge-change path** (`src/rebar/review_bot/`): detect merge changes via `revisions/{rev}/commit` parent count; assemble review input from `files/` (no `parent` → auto-merge base) + per-file `diff` (auto-merge base) + `mergelist` context; NEVER bare `/patch` on merges (assert the 409 path stays fail-closed if hit); merge-context size-capped like DIFF_CHAR_CAP; robot comment states merge-change mode + integrated-commit count; `reconcile.py` backfill and `/rerun` work for merge changes; fail-closed preserved end-to-end; unit + fixture tests for all of the above.
- [ ] **S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes** (assumes 1fa8 live): prove g2p hook dispatch + `gerrit-verify.yaml` (checkout-gerrit-change-action fetching the merge refspec) work for (a) changes targeting `feature/*`, (b) merge changes into main — CI builds the actual merge tree; Verified re-runs (not carries) on a re-merge; `concurrency: cancel-in-progress` behaves sanely across branch + merge changes; document any g2p branch-filter config required.
- [ ] **S4 — Documentation sweep**: `CONTRIBUTING.md` gains a "Multi-story features (feature branches)" section (branch creation request path, story loop targeting the branch, catch-up merge recipe, merge-back recipe incl. the Change-Id amend, pushMerge-refusal troubleshooting, branch deletion); `CLAUDE.md` Git-workflow section (~line 410) gains the feature-branch flow + when-agents-should-use-it guidance (multi-agent parallel features vs single small changes); `README.md` + install docs (`docs/local-dev-env.md`, CONTRIBUTING one-time setup) verified current for the two-vote + feature-branch world; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`; `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` (merge-change ops: 409 semantics, /rerun on merges); repo-wide sweep for stale text implying the single-change amend loop is the ONLY flow or describing PR-era GitHub practices (grep "pull request", "gh pr", "PR merge", single-change-only phrasing) — every hit fixed or justified.
- [ ] **S5 — Live multi-agent E2E validation** on the running Gerrit (scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`; every scenario's change URLs + votes recorded on this ticket): (1) **parallel siblings** — two agents in separate worktrees push independent story changes to `feature/e2e-<date>`; both earn LLM-Review + Verified and submit WITHOUT either rebasing; (2) **stacking** — a third agent pulls the branch (both stories in history) and pushes a change on top; the reviewed diff covers ONLY its delta; (3) **merge-back** — `--no-ff` merge change to main: bot reviews the auto-merge delta only (assert NOT the whole-feature diff), CI green on the merge tree, both votes present, submit lands atomically, replication advances GitHub main with all commits, branch deleted; (4) **vote-carry on re-merge** — land an unrelated change on main mid-flight, push a re-merge (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE): LLM-Review +1 CARRIES, Verified RE-RUNS; then change the feature tip: BOTH wiped (REWORK); (5) **textual conflict** — overlapping feature-vs-main edits resolved in the merge commit; bot reviews the non-empty auto-merge delta; (6) **semantic conflict** — textually-clean but semantically broken combination (e.g. rename on main + new call-site on feature): auto-merge diff empty, LLM-Review passes, CI red → Verified=-1 BLOCKS submit (proves two-vote complementarity); (7) **races + negatives** — concurrent submits into the branch behave (MERGE_IF_NECESSARY or clear conflict signal); a merge push WITHOUT pushMerge is refused; branch creation outside the authorized group is refused; the reconciler backfills a dropped webhook for a feature-branch change.
- [ ] **S6 — Process + back-out**: branch-lifetime cap + catch-up cadence documented; back-out documented + tested (revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes); cost/latency notes recorded (re-reviews avoided vs CI re-runs incurred).

## Success Criteria
- R1–R5 all hold end-to-end and are each demonstrated by at least one S5 scenario (R1: scenarios 2+3; R2: 3; R3: 1+3+6; R4: 1+4; R5: recorded OSS evidence in apt-otter-urn + ADR).
- The RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (scenarios 1–3) completes with zero vote wipes, zero rebases, zero re-reviews of already-attested stories, and a single atomic landing on main.
- No repo documentation describes a stale GitHub/Gerrit practice (S4 sweep clean).

## Sequencing & risk
S1 (additive config) → S2 (bot merge path MUST precede any real merge change — today a merge change 409s to a fail-closed -1) → S3 (needs 1fa8 live) → S4 (after semantics locked) → S5 (last; gates epic close) ; S6 folds into S4/S5 outputs. Risk: auto-merge review blindness to semantic conflicts is accepted and mitigated BY DESIGN via the Verified re-run posture (S3) + scenario 6 proof. Risk: long-lived branches rot — mitigated by lifetime cap + catch-up cadence (S6).

## Out of scope
- Cherry-pick-down release-branch model (Qt QUIP-16) — solves release maintenance, not feature accumulation.
- Automated feature-branch cleanup service (scripted/manual deletion only).
- Changes to relation-chain/topic behavior for SMALL work (a 2–3 change chain remains fine; feature branches are for multi-story features).
- Any GitHub-side workflow (the mirror stays read-only; PRs stay dead).
- Stacked-diff client tooling.

## Key references
- Validation + research record: session log apt-otter-urn (c905-a38c-0419-448e) — OSS citations, REST mechanics, corrections.
- OpenDev Infra Manual "Project Driver's Guide" (feature branches + merge-back recipe + pushMerge ACL); openstack/swift feature/mpu live changes; Qt Merge Guidelines + QUIP-16.
- Gerrit 3.x docs: config-labels.html (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE), rest-api-changes.html (files/diff parent semantics, mergelist, GetPatch 409), access-control.html (pushMerge, Create/Delete Reference), user-review-ui.html (auto-merge base, Merge List), config-project-config.html (rejectImplicitMerges exempts real merges).
- Local: infra/gerrit/{project.config,setup-project.sh,reviewbot-e2e.sh}, src/rebar/review_bot/{voter.py,gerrit_client.py,adapter.py,reconcile.py}, ADR-0010/0013, epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) + its ADRs, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md §Git workflow, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md.
data.title: Feature-branch accumulation: multi-story features off-main under per-story LLM+CI gates, landing via a reviewed merge change
